title: Bounded storage lifecycle for Heim-PC operator artifacts
goal: Stop uncontrolled growth of temporary worktrees, build outputs, RepoBrief publications, caches and Docker residue by binding every large regenerable artifact to identity, owner, budget, retention and a safe cleanup lifecycle.
completion: New temporary storage producers are inventory-bound and budgeted before they can grow without limit.
Automated repository worktrees do not retain large local build outputs after task completion.
RepoBrief publication is a no-op for an identical source identity and retains only bounded history plus explicit pins.
Checkout cleanup remains dirty-state, task, process, lease, archive and dry-run guarded.
Storage growth and cleanup outcomes are visible as machine-readable time series and in Leitstand.

metadata.safety_boundary: Stop new growth before reclaiming historical data. No dirty worktree, active task, live process, foreign lease, unverified Docker volume or unpinned evidence artifact may be removed.
does_not_establish: safe cleanup without fresh live readback
metadata.dependency_closeout_basis: T008 was verified from an exact hash-bound Docker apply, two recovery-archived checkout cleanup plans, full producer/checkout classification, post-effect inventories and refreshed artifact-backed Leitstand readback. T009 was released as the read-only 14-day observation phase and cannot be meaningfully reviewed before 2026-07-29T19:39:20.012Z.
